summary: Implemented the desktop table state polish in the presentation-client renderer so the live game surface exposes phase, active actor, contract context, declarer, trump when applicable, and current trick context inside the existing desktop rail without changing domain legality or scoring behavior. Added focused renderer coverage for contract-family, contract-play, all-pass, and misere state visibility rules, while preserving the score/result surfaces from the US-844 dependency. The resulting UI keeps the first screen as a playable game surface and makes state comprehension clearer across bidding, contract, play, settlement, next-deal, and finished flows.

tests_run.evidence: 20 renderer tests passed after adding the new state-context assertions for contract/order, whist, contract play, all-pass, and misere states.

tests_run.command: npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts
tests_run.evidence: 36 tests passed across renderer, controller shell behavior, rule-driven flow, and score/result integration.
Vitest completed 13 files / 128 tests with 0 failures, then tsc and vite build completed successfully.
The local Vite desktop app started successfully at http://127.0.0.1:5331/ for live UI inspection.

verification_environment.verification_scope: presentation-client desktop state rendering, focused UI/integration regression, and full test/build verification

node_package_scripts.verify: npm run test && npm run build

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop table shows current phase, active actor, legal human actions, contract, trump when applicable, current trick, hands, score table, deal result, and final result.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: src/ui/render.ts; src/styles.css; src/ui/render.test.ts; npm run test -- src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/ruleFlow.integration.test.ts src/test/us844Scoring.integration.test.ts; npm run verify
evidence_matrix.limitations: Fresh browser screenshot capture was not added in this task report; runtime evidence is the verified renderer DOM/CSS plus targeted and full automated test/build coverage.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop/laptop bidding, contract, whist, play, deal-result, and final-result states remain understandable and do not block normal play.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Deal-result visibility remains understandable and the player can proceed to the next deal unless the bullet is complete.

evidence_matrix.limitations: Coverage is representative rather than exhaustive for every phase permutation, but it explicitly exercises contract/order, whist, contract play, all-pass, and misere desktop contexts.

evidence_matrix.scenario: UI legal-action projection continues to depend on upstream rule legality and phase transition contracts without changing rule behavior in this task.
